title: Retry loop double-counts HTTP attempts when status is not retryable on the first attempt
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
error-handling
## Location
src/pipeline.rs:330-367
## Violation
The retry loop in `run_turn` has a subtle control-flow defect. The non-retry success path issues `response = Some(resp); break;`, but the failure path for `!is_success()` for a non-retryable status returns Err immediately even on attempts < max_retries. Worse, the retry exponent uses `2u64.pow(attempt + 1)` so on attempt 0 the wait is 2s, attempt 1 is 4s, attempt 2 is 8s — but the loop is `for attempt in 0..=max_retries` (4 iterations) and the last attempt cannot retry. The result is the user sees `Retrying in 16s — ...` printed for an attempt that will not actually retry:

```rust
for attempt in 0..=max_retries {        // 0,1,2,3 (4 iterations)
    ...
    if matches!(status, 429 | 503 | 529) && attempt < max_retries {
        let wait_secs = ... .unwrap_or_else(|| 2u64.pow(attempt + 1));
        ...
        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_secs(wait_secs)).await;
        continue;
    }
```
The user-visible retry message also leaks before `tokio::time::sleep` actually waits — the message displays the *next* attempt's wait, not the current. There is no jitter, so two concurrent requests hitting 429 will retry in lockstep and amplify the rate limit.
## Mandated refactor
Use `0..max_retries` to bound iterations clearly, decouple retry decision from message emission, add jitter (`rand::thread_rng().gen_range(0..wait_secs/4)`), and surface a structured RetryEvent rather than streaming free-form text into the model output buffer. Cap total elapsed retry time, not just attempt count.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
